rulings.Case Title: LATAYA ROBINSON VS. CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 2023.010, 2023.030 And 2032.240
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 7, 2025, line 1, 1 - DEFENDANTS CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER, INC., HAMLIN HOTEL 2019, LP'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Court Order And For Sanctions Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Sections 2023.010, 2023.030 And 2032.240 *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* Defendant's motion is denied. The court grants Robinson's request to file her opposition late in light of the CCP 473(b) declaration she has provided. The court finds that CCDC has acted with substantial justification in bringing this motion and does not award sanctions to Robinson. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CV)

rulings.Case Title: 2075 SUTTER STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. EAST BAY CONSTRUCTION & MANAGEMENT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Liver Ramiro Guzman To Comply With Deposition Subpoena, To Appear For His Deposition And To Produce Subpoenaed Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 7, 2025, line 2, PLAINTIFF 2075 SUTTER STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Motion To Compel Liver Ramiro Guzman To Comply With Deposition Subpoena, To Appear For His Deposition And To Produce Subpoenaed Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Plaintiff 2075 Sutter Street HOA moves for an order compelling compliance with its deposition subpoena to Liver Ramiro Guzman and for sanctions. No opposition was filed. Plaintiff has shown that the deposition subpoena was properly served and that Guzman did not appear. Plaintiff has shown further that it attempted to contact Guzman to inquire about the nonappearance and that it served Guzman with its motion papers. For good cause shown in Plaintiff's moving papers, the court grants the motion. Guzman is ordered to attend and testify at a rescheduled deposition, and to produce for inspection any document, electronically stored information, or tangible thing described in the deposition notice. The rescheduled deposition shall be noticed for a date within 15 days of notice of entry of this order, but Guzman must be given at least 10 days' notice of the rescheduled deposition. The Guzman and the parties to the litigation may mutually agree to extend the time for deposition for scheduling convenience. Plaintiff seeks sanctions. CCP 2025.450(g)(1) provides, "If a motion under subdivision (a) is granted, the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . in favor of the party who noticed the deposition and against the deponent or the party with whom the deponent is affiliated, unless the court finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court finds Guzman has not acted with substantial justification. The court orders sanctions payable by Guzman to Plaintiff; sanctions shall be paid within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: EVOLUTION CAPITAL PARTNERS III LLC, A DELAWARE VS. ORACLE CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Seal Msj Opposition Materials
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, February 7, 2025, line 1, 3 - DEFENDANTS ORACLE CORPORATION, FACQUISITION CORP'S. Motion To Seal Msj Opposition Materials Defendant's motion to seal is denied without prejudice. A court can seal a record only if: "(1) There exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) The overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) A substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) The proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) No less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest." (Rule of Court 2.550(d).) Defendant fails to make that showing. Defendant argues that its contractual obligation not to disclose and confidential business information constitute an overriding interest to warrant sealing. But "[t]he second element of the overriding interest analysis is there must be a substantial probability that it will be prejudiced absent closure or sealing." (Universal City Studios, Inc. v. Superior Court, Inc. (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1273, 1283.) Here, there is no explanation how defendant will be prejudiced if the garden variety notice, indemnity, and choice of law provisions are disclosed. The motion is also not narrowly tailored. For example, it is unclear how plaintiff's mere assertion at UMF 13 that "[t]he Oracle Defendants concede that the Escrow Agreement and the Purchase Agreement are unambiguous" needs to be sealed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CV)

rulings.Case Title: LLW VENTURES LLC VS. PROMAX BUILDERS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants To Produce Code-Compliant Discovery Responses, To Produce Documents, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 7, 2025, line 3, 2 - PLAINTIFF LLW VENTURES LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Defendants To Produce Code-Compliant Discovery Responses, To Produce Documents, And Request For Sanctions Plaintiff's motion is denied for failure to comply with CRC 3.1345. Parties are ordered to meet and confer by February 28, 2025, regarding the discovery issues raised in the above motions. Plaintiff's motion to file a further motion to compel is extended until March 17, 2025. Sanctions are generally mandatory when a party unsuccessfully makes or opposes a discovery motion unless the party acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make the imposition of sanctions unjust. The court finds that both parties acted without substantial justification here and does not award sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: LLW VENTURES LLC VS. PROMAX BUILDERS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Discovery Stay
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, February 7, 2025, line 3, 1 - DEFENDANT MICHAEL YIN, JEFFREY DRAEGER, MARCELLA MANCE, PROMAX BUILDERS, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order And Discovery Stay Defendants' motion for protective order and stay is denied. A party seeking a protective order must show good cause for issuance of the order by a preponderance of evidence. (Stadish v. Sup.Ct. (Southern Calif. Gas Co.) (1999) 71 CA4th 1130, 1145.) Defendants argue that the propounded discovery calls for production of broad categories of Defendants' and unnamed third parties' personal, corporate, financial, commercial, contractual, and business records that are irrelevant, and notes privacy and other rights are implicated. (Mot., 9:7-10.) Plaintiff argues the RFPDs solely seek information in support of the allegations in the Complaint and do not call for financial condition discovery. (Opp. 13:2-4.) Defendants' vague and conclusory statements concerning the potential for private information contained in documents for which it has concededly not yet conducted a search fail to demonstrate good cause for a protective order by a preponderance of the evidence. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL ESPINOZA VS. AQUA-LEISURE RECREATION, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, February 7, 2025, line 4, PLAINTIFF GABRIEL ESPINOZA'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. Health and Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (f)(4) provides: "If there is a settlement of an action brought by a person in the public interest under subdivision (d), the plaintiff shall submit the settlement, other than a voluntary dismissal in which no consideration is received from the defendant, to the court for approval upon noticed motion, and the court may approve the settlement only if the court makes all of the following findings: (A) The warning that is required by the settlement complies with this chapter. (B) The award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. (C) The penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in paragraph (2) of subdivision (b)." The court finds that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CV)
